title: anthemis punctata subsp. kabylica
intro: Anthemis punctata subsp. kabylica is a subspecies of the Anthemis punctata plant, a type of daisy-like flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is commonly found in mountainous regions of North Africa, where it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive health. Anthemis punctata subsp. kabylica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health purposes.
